examples: In our body, some food is indigestible.
The leaves of an oak tree taste foul because they are rich in tannins, a chemical that renders them distasteful or indigestible to many organisms.
Many Kosovars would be happy to be shot of their indigestible north.
As a result, says the report, the government faces an "indigestible menu of short-term policy choices".
And it is being sold with a heavy dose of sugar-coated earnestness that many non-Americans may find indigestible.
